channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: As Iran War Reaches 60-Day Mark, Republicans in Congress Grow Impatient With Trump
item.description: Months into the operation and with midterms looming, some lawmakers are calling for Congress to restrain the president’s war power or set terms for bringing the conflict to a close.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The defense secretary and the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff testified before both chambers of Congress at the Capitol this week.

item.title: Trump ‘Not Satisfied’ With Iran’s Proposal on Ending War
item.description: The details of the latest plan were unclear, and President Trump did not specify his objections.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A government-organized march in Tehran on Wednesday in support of Mojtaba Khamenei, the supreme leader.

item.title: Jared Kushner, Steve Witkoff and the Profitable Business of Peace
item.description: The Trump administration is turning diplomacy into an asset to be leveraged.
item.dc:creator: Linda Kinstler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Israel Said to Have Helped Defend Emirates in Iran War With Iron Dome
item.description: It appeared to be the first time the missile defense system was sent to an Arab country. The Emirates bore the brunt of Iran’s retaliatory attacks on Gulf countries during the war.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Leo Correa/Associated Press
item.media:description: Israel’s Iron Dome air defense system intercepting missiles over Tel Aviv, Israel, in June.

item.title: FEMA Disaster Aid is Flowing Slowly In Trump’s Second Term
item.description: During President Trump’s second term, the disaster declarations that unlock money are taking longer than in the past. Blue states wait the longest and they hear ‘no’ more often.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance and Ashley Cai
item.media:credit: Alonzo Adams/Associated Press
item.media:description: A damaged home in Enid, Okla., on April 24, after a tornado ripped through the night before.

item.title: Obamacare Enrollment Drops Sharply as Costs Rise
item.description: Americans can’t afford the higher health insurance premiums that resulted from Congress’s refusal to extend federal tax credits.
item.dc:creator: Reed Abelson and Margot Sanger-Katz
item.category: Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (2010)
Blue Cross and Blue Shield Assn
item.media:credit: Lauren-Pruitt/The New York Times

item.title: Pentagon Makes Deals With A.I. Companies to Expand Classified Work
item.description: The agreements with the technology companies come amid the Defense Department’s dispute with Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Sheera Frenkel
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Anthropic and the Pentagon have been locked in a debate over whether the company’s A.I. model could be used to pilot autonomous drones or work on domestic surveillance.

item.title: F.D.A. Grants Early Access to Promising Drug for Pancreatic Cancer
item.description: Patients with one of the deadliest cancers have been pleading for an unapproved treatment that may prolong their lives.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins and Gina Kolata
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: “Every month that that drug is delayed means that I have to get chemo two more times,” said Dr. Ronald Silvestri, 77, a retired pulmonologist who has metastatic pancreatic cancer.

item.title: After Prison, a Financial Titan Plots an Unlikely Comeback
item.description: Bill McGlashan served time for trying to buy his son’s way into college during the Varsity Blues scandal. He hopes his new venture will restore his name — and save the planet.
item.dc:creator: David Gelles
item.media:credit: Laura Morton for The New York Times
item.media:description: Bill McGlashan, co-founder of the start-up Oath, which uses a blend of microscopic organisms to improve yield and quality of food crops, reducing water and fertilizer use, near the company’s headquarters in San Francisco.

item.title: Spirit Airlines Prepares to Shut Down After Trump Administration Bailout Falls Through
item.description: The low-fare airline, which has struggled for years, had been trying to secure a $500 million lifeline from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch and Niraj Chokshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Wilfredo Lee/Associated Press
item.media:description: Spirit Airlines will stop flying after failing to find a way to emerge from bankruptcy in a time of high fuel prices and stiff competition.

item.title: Spirit Airlines May Be Shutting Down. Here’s What to Do if You’re Scheduled to Fly.
item.description: After two stints in bankruptcy, the low-cost airline appears close to shutting down. Here’s what travelers should know about rebooking and refunds if Spirit stops flying.
item.dc:creator: Seth Kugel
item.media:credit: Scott Olson/Getty Images
item.media:description: A proposed $500 million government bailout of Spirit Airlines, squeezed by spiking jet fuel prices, appears to have fallen apart, leaving the airline near a shutdown.

item.title: Trump Administration Pushes Forward With Tariffs Based on Forced Labor Laws
item.description: Nonprofit groups have applauded an investigation on trade in goods made with forced labor but say that the Trump administration should go further to enforce its own laws
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Cotton picked in the Chinese region of Xinjiang at a rail station in Jiujiang, China. A U.S. law bans most products from Xinjiang, but some have called on the United States to do more to enforce it.

item.title: Stocks and Oil Prices Sent Conflicting Signals in April Amid Havoc of Iran War
item.description: Energy markets remained disrupted by turmoil in the Middle East, while the S&P 500 posted its best month since November 2020.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump’s Tariff Reversal Reopens a Major Market for Scotch Whisky
item.description: A 10 percent tax on American imports was hurting British sales, even though exporters had increased sales in India, China and other countries.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Emily Whitney for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Scottish whisky industry said it had been losing millions of pounds a week because of the American import tax.

item.title: 6 Common Medications That May Decrease Dementia Risk
item.description: Some vaccines, along with heart medications and other drugs, appear to have a protective benefit.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith

item.title: 4 Medications That May Increase Dementia Risk
item.description: Experts have flagged several over-the-counter and prescription medications.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith

item.title: U.S. Indictment of Sinaloa’s Governor Is a Reckoning for Residents of the Mexican State
item.description: The U.S. indictment of a Mexican governor confirms what many residents say they had long suspected: The line between organized crime and the upper echelons of government has blurred.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas
item.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of the Mexican National Guard in Culiacán, Sinaloa, in February.

item.title: Voters Sue Over Louisiana Governor’s Move to Delay Primary
item.description: Legal challenges are mounting over the decision to suspend the state’s May House primary after a Supreme Court ruling found that the congressional map was unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane and Nick Corasaniti
item.media:credit: Hilary Scheinuk/The Advocate, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Gov. Jeff Landry, a Republican from Louisiana, suspended the state’s House primary, which was scheduled for May 16, to draw new congressional districts. Voting rights groups have sued.

item.title: ‘The Most Bipartisan Issue Since Beer’: Opposition to Data Centers
item.description: Americans have soured on data centers, polls show, and the sentiment is profoundly bipartisan. How will that change our politics?
item.dc:creator: Sabrina Tavernise

item.title: Maryland Is First to Ban A.I.-Driven Price Increases in Grocery Stores
item.description: A state law that goes into effect in October prohibits grocery stores and third-party delivery services from using consumer data to boost prices.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald
item.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Maryland bill comes amid increasing pushback against so-called dynamic pricing in states across America.

item.title: In the Oregon Governor’s Primary, Voters Choose the Future of the Republican Party
item.description: Oregon’s Democratic governor is likely to win re-election, but how voters pick from the ideological array of Republicans challenging her could offer a glimpse at the party post-Trump.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin and Jordan Gale
item.media:credit: Jordan Gale for The New York Times

item.title: Jury Convicts Florida Ex-Rep. David Rivera in Conspiracy Trial
item.description: The nation’s state-run oil company hired David Rivera’s consulting firm for $50 million to influence members of Congress and the White House.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams and Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Marco Bello/Reuters
item.media:description: Former U.S. Representative David Rivera at the James Lawrence King Federal Justice Building in Miami in March.

item.title: Can the Costume Institute Survive Without the Met Gala?
item.description: The Metropolitan Museum of Art and the party that has supported its fashion wing may be entering a new era.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by Joan Wong; Gary Gershoff/WireImage (Bolton), Taylor Hill/Getty Images (Wintour)

item.title: With ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ and the Met Gala, Fashion Fans May Get Déjà Vu
item.description: That’s “meta” with a lowercase m, by the way. After a weekslong promotional blitz for “The Devil Wears Prada 2,” the scene on Monday may feel very familiar.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Macall Polay/20th Century Studios, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Meryl Streep and Stanley Tucci in an early scene from “The Devil Wears Prada 2” that evokes a distinctly Met Gala-like affair.

item.title: Beyoncé’s Best Met Gala Looks as She Returns to the Event After 10 Years
item.description: After 10 years away from the event, the superstar will return as a co-chair on Monday. Here’s what she has worn to her seven past gala appearances.
item.dc:creator: Gina Cherelus
item.media:credit: Getty Images

item.title: This Is What’s Behind Trump’s Relentlessness
item.description: “Animal spirits” and American presidents.
item.dc:creator: Jackson Lears and John Guida
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times

item.title: Profound Autism Is Difficult Enough Without This Debunked Method
item.description: Study after study has called facilitated communication into question.
item.dc:creator: Amy S.F. Lutz

item.title: The Voting Rights Act
item.description: We explain the significance of the Supreme Court’s decision.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times

item.title: Why Mayor Mamdani Didn’t Roll Out the Red Carpet for the Royals
item.description: Zohran Mamdani studiously avoided the type of red-carpet welcome most mayors would prepare for a royal visit, reflecting his upbringing but also drawing some criticism.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
item.media:description: Former Mayor Michael R. Bloomberg, left, escorted King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the 9/11 Memorial, rather than New York City’s current mayor, Zohran Mamdani.

item.title: Myanmar’s Junta Claims ‘Benevolence’ for Aung San Suu Kyi. It Still Rules Cruelly.
item.description: Moving the deposed civilian leader to a “designated residence” for the rest of her prison sentence is part of an effort by Myanmar’s regime to portray legitimacy.
item.dc:creator: Richard C. Paddock
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
item.media:credit: Myanmar Military Information Team, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: On Thursday, the state-run Myanmar Military Information Team released an image of Aung San Suu Kyi in an undisclosed location.

item.title: In Permissive Amsterdam, Ads for Fossil Fuels or Meat Are Now Verboden
item.description: The Dutch city has outlawed advertising that promotes lifestyles linked to high carbon emissions, which is a driver of climate change. It’s a first for a world capital.
item.dc:creator: Cara Buckley

item.title: Small Plane Crashes in Texas, Killing 5
item.description: Emergency personnel responded to the crash site near Wimberley late Thursday night, officials said. The victims have not yet been publicly identified.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz
item.media:credit: Alex Kent for The New York Times
item.media:description: Officials were investigating after a Cessna 421C crashed in Texas on Thursday night.
